title: Akola Fort
tagline: Akola Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Akola Fort was built by Govind Appaji in 780 AD.
overview.description: Akola Fort was built by Govind Appaji in 780 AD. It also called Korbandh before invaded by Asad khan, Later name was changed to Asadgadh in 1697 along with the Narnala and Akot forts forms the major fortifications of the Akola district Former name Beraar Province, Maharashtra, India.

topPlaces.name: Akola
topPlaces.description: Akola () is a major city in the Vidarbha region of the Indian state of Maharashtra. With an estimated population of 600,000, Akola is a growing metropolis surrounded by forts, and has the Morna River running through the center of the city.

topPlaces.name: Akola Cricket Club Ground
topPlaces.description: Akola Cricket Club Ground is a cricket ground in Akola, Vidarbha, India. The ground has held two first-class matches, the first of which came in the 1983/84 Ranji Trophy when Vidarbha played the Railways, while the second saw Rajasthan as the visitors in the 1984/85 Ranji Trophy.

topPlaces.name: Umri Pragane Balapur
topPlaces.description: Umri Pragane Balapur is a census town in Akola district in the Indian state of Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Malkapur, Akola
topPlaces.description: Malkapur is a census town in Akola district in the Indian state of Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Dr. Panjabrao Deshmukh Krishi Vidyapeeth
topPlaces.description: Dr. Panjabrao Deshmukh Krishi Vidyapeeth (PDKV or PKV) is an agricultural university located at Akola, in Maharashtra, India, in the Vidarbha region.

items.note: Akola () is a major city in the Vidarbha region of the Indian state of Maharashtra. With an estimated population of 600,000, Akola is a growing metropolis surrounded by forts, and has the Morna River running through the center of the city.

Akola Cricket Club Ground is a cricket ground in Akola, Vidarbha, India. The ground has held two first-class matches, the first of which came in the 1983/84 Ranji Trophy when Vidarbha played the Railways, while the second saw Rajasthan as the visitors in the 1984/85 Ranji Trophy.
Umri Pragane Balapur is a census town in Akola district in the Indian state of Maharashtra.
Malkapur is a census town in Akola district in the Indian state of Maharashtra.
Dr. Panjabrao Deshmukh Krishi Vidyapeeth (PDKV or PKV) is an agricultural university located at Akola, in Maharashtra, India, in the Vidarbha region.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Akola Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Akola Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Akola Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jalgaon Airport (145 km) and the nearest railway station is Aurangabad Railway Station (197 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Akola Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Akola Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Akola Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Akola Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Akola Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Akola, Akola Cricket Club Ground, Umri Pragane Balapur, Malkapur, Akola, Dr. Panjabrao Deshmukh Krishi Vidyapeeth.
